historical_notes: US industrial-policy legislation to support domestic semiconductor capacity and reduce strategic supply-chain vulnerability
coding_notes: Included as V2 anchor event linking industrial policy to US-China strategic competition; already adjacent to V1 dataset
analyst_caveat: Legislation is broad and highly anticipated; market interpretation should not be reduced to a single event-day reaction

event_name: BIS advanced computing and semiconductor manufacturing export controls
historical_notes: US controls targeted advanced computing items and semiconductor manufacturing capability associated with China-related strategic competition
coding_notes: Included as core export-control event; use rule announcement date as event date
analyst_caveat: Controls include multiple technical provisions; downstream firm exposure requires separate asset coding

event_name: YMTC and other Chinese entities added to Entity List
historical_notes: Entity-list action against Chinese technology entities including a memory-chip firm expands the named-target restriction channel
coding_notes: Included as sanctions/entity-list example adjacent to existing V1 technology-restriction coverage
analyst_caveat: Entity List is not identical to financial sanctions; code as restriction on named technology entities

historical_notes: Japan announced controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment in a move relevant to allied technology-control alignment
coding_notes: Included because US-China competition often operates through allied control alignment; geography is Japan/China rather than direct US/China
analyst_caveat: Allied-control event; use caution when comparing directly with US-origin controls

event_name: China restricts Micron products in critical information infrastructure procurement
historical_notes: China cybersecurity review result restricted Micron products for critical information infrastructure operators and illustrates retaliation/procurement-risk channel
coding_notes: Included as China-side named-company restriction in US-China technology competition
analyst_caveat: Procurement restriction is not a market forecast; company impact depends on exposure and implementation

event_name: China announces gallium and germanium export controls
historical_notes: China announced export controls on gallium and germanium items relevant to semiconductor and defense supply chains
coding_notes: Included as critical-minerals restriction adjacent to semiconductor and strategic-technology competition
analyst_caveat: Direct market effect depends on material exposure and licensing implementation

event_name: US executive order on outbound investment in sensitive technologies
historical_notes: US executive order established restrictions or notification requirements for outbound investment in sensitive technologies involving countries of concern
coding_notes: Included as strategic investment-screening anchor event; directly fits V2 pilot scope
analyst_caveat: Executive order sets framework; final implementation details may differ

event_name: BIS updates advanced computing and semiconductor export controls
historical_notes: US updated controls on advanced computing items and semiconductor manufacturing items to limit advanced computing capability transfer
coding_notes: Included as AI-chip restriction event and update to 2022 controls
analyst_caveat: Named firms are exposed through product categories; event is not a recommendation or expected-return signal

historical_notes: China announced export controls on certain graphite items relevant to batteries and strategic supply chains
coding_notes: Included as critical-minerals and battery-supply-chain example within US-China strategic competition
analyst_caveat: Graphite controls affect broader battery supply chains and are less semiconductor-adjacent than gallium/germanium

event_name: China restricts export of rare-earth processing technology
historical_notes: China restricted export of rare-earth extraction and processing technology in a strategic minerals context
coding_notes: Included as strategic critical-minerals technology-control event; source is major media because official catalog sourcing is harder to normalize
analyst_caveat: Use source caveat; event coding should be reviewed against official catalog before production use

event_name: TSMC Arizona CHIPS preliminary terms announced
historical_notes: US preliminary CHIPS Act terms with TSMC Arizona support supply-chain relocation and domestic advanced-node capacity
coding_notes: Included as supply-chain relocation and industrial-capacity case adjacent to V1 semiconductor support events
analyst_caveat: Support terms are preliminary and firm-specific; avoid generalizing to all supply-chain relocation

event_name: China semiconductor Big Fund III registered
historical_notes: China registered a large third semiconductor investment fund to support domestic chip capability
coding_notes: Included as China-side industrial-policy counterpart to US semiconductor support; source is major media for registration report
analyst_caveat: Review official registration documents before production coding; market channel is strategic capacity rather than immediate firm event

event_name: China launches Taiwan-related military drills after inauguration period
historical_notes: China military drills around Taiwan created geopolitical tension relevant to semiconductor supply-chain and regional-risk analysis
coding_notes: Included only as one Taiwan-related tension subcategory within broader US-China competition pilot
analyst_caveat: Do not frame this pilot as Taiwan-focused; event does not create direct market-reaction evidence without separate asset coding

event_name: US Treasury finalizes outbound investment rule for China-related technologies
historical_notes: US Treasury issued final regulations implementing outbound investment restrictions and notification requirements for certain technologies involving countries of concern
coding_notes: Included as implementation follow-through for strategic investment screening; related to V2P007
analyst_caveat: Potential duplicate relationship with executive order should be tracked as implementation milestone rather than separate policy family

event_name: BIS announces controls on advanced computing and semiconductor manufacturing items including HBM
historical_notes: US announced additional controls on advanced computing and semiconductor manufacturing items including high-bandwidth memory relevant to AI systems
coding_notes: Included as newer AI-chip and semiconductor-manufacturing restriction for future V2 expansion; not wired into V1 workflow
analyst_caveat: Check final rule text before production use; current row is pilot evidence for schema-governed expansion
